skill.name: modelarts-skill
skill.description: Use Huawei Cloud ModelArts from Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to manage notebooks, training jobs, inference services, resource pools, VPC data, KMS keypairs, SWR images, and Lite Server resources.
skill.summary: Manage Huawei Cloud ModelArts resources through authenticated SDK and API helper functions.
skill.author: modelarts-agent

security_audit.summary: The critical static findings for obfuscation, Windows SAM access, and weak cryptography appear to be false positives caused by documentation text, domain terms, and compact Python expressions. The skill is still high risk because it handles Huawei Cloud credentials, makes signed cloud API requests, manages destructive cloud resources, and can submit user-provided commands to ModelArts training jobs. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found in the reviewed files.

high_findings.title: Cloud credentials are loaded and used for privileged sessions
high_findings.description: The skill reads AK/SK, project, region, and security token values from ModelArts sessions or caller input, then creates authenticated sessions. This is legitimate for ModelArts automation, but compromise or prompt misuse could act with the user account permissions.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly accesses and stores cloud credential fields and passes them into a ModelArts Session. The reviewed comments indicate masking intent, so this is high operational risk rather than confirmed exfiltration.

high_findings.title: User-provided commands can be submitted to cloud training jobs
high_findings.description: Training job creation accepts a command string and places it into the ModelArts job algorithm configuration before sending it to the cloud API. This enables expected ML job execution, but it can run arbitrary caller-supplied commands in provisioned cloud training environments.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command parameter is part of the public function signature and is copied into the training job request body. I did not find evidence of local shell execution, but remote command execution is an explicit capability.

high_findings.title: Authorization management can submit AK/SK credentials and alter access
high_findings.description: The authorization module can add credential-based authorizations, include a secret key in the request body, and delete authorizations. These are sensitive administrative actions that require trusted callers and careful permission boundaries.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The function explicitly accepts secret_key for CREDENTIAL authorization and sends it through the authenticated API helper. The behavior is documented and probably intended, but it is highly sensitive.

medium_findings.title: Signed network requests to Huawei Cloud services
medium_findings.description: The skill constructs Huawei Cloud service endpoints and sends signed HTTP requests for KMS, VPC, and common ModelArts API operations. The destinations are service endpoints, but network access plus credentials creates data exposure and account-impact risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The requests are sent to Huawei Cloud endpoints with signed headers and SDK credentials. This appears legitimate for the skill purpose, not an unknown exfiltration endpoint.

medium_findings.title: Broad cloud resource lifecycle operations
medium_findings.description: The skill includes functions for creating pools, deleting authorizations, starting and stopping notebooks, and managing Lite Server resources. These operations are legitimate for MLOps, but they can incur cost or disrupt workloads if invoked incorrectly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The module exports and implements lifecycle operations against cloud resources. This is expected for a ModelArts management skill, but the blast radius is larger than a read-only helper.

medium_findings.title: Path insertion and module bootstrapping increase local import risk
medium_findings.description: Several modules insert local directories into sys.path before importing shared helpers. This supports the package layout, but it can load unintended local modules if the runtime directory is not trusted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path manipulation is visible and likely used for local module resolution. The risk depends on whether untrusted files can be placed earlier in the import path.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks were misclassified as Ruby shell execution
low_findings.description: Many external command findings are false positives in reference tables and Markdown examples. The cited lines contain inline code formatting and function names, not Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are documentation tables and inline references. They do not execute commands.

low_findings.title: Critical Windows SAM and weak crypto flags are naming false positives
low_findings.description: Static critical and weak cryptography findings appear to match words such as SECURITY, SDK wrapper names, model fields, and domain-specific identifiers. The reviewed examples do not access the Windows SAM database or implement weak cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are comments, function signatures, and response mapping code. No evidence of SAM file access, cryptographic primitives, or obfuscated execution was found in these locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs and IPs are service examples or cloud endpoints
low_findings.description: Hardcoded endpoint and IP findings are mainly Huawei Cloud service endpoints or documented network examples. They should still be reviewed for region correctness, but I did not find evidence of malicious external collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoints are constructed for Huawei Cloud services, and the IP is shown in a security group example. These are not sufficient evidence of malicious networking.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential-backed API session creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill creates sessions with access key, secret key, project, region, and optional security token values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The session constructor is given credential fields directly. This is required for the integration but raises account-impact risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote command field in training job payload
dangerous_patterns.description: The training configuration builder copies caller-provided command values into the cloud job algorithm payload.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code path from command input to job configuration is direct and intentional.

dangerous_patterns.title: Signed HTTP request dispatch
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill signs requests with Huawei Cloud credentials and dispatches them over HTTPS.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Network dispatch is explicit and credential-backed. The destinations appear to be expected Huawei Cloud services.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local sys.path mutation before imports
dangerous_patterns.description: The code mutates sys.path to load local modules, which can be risky in an untrusted working directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present, but exploitability depends on filesystem trust and module placement.

content.value_statement: ModelArts teams need fast access to notebooks, training jobs, inference services, pools, storage, and network resources. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured helpers for authenticated ModelArts operations.
actual_capabilities: Routes ModelArts tasks to notebook, training, inference, pool, node pool, management, SWR, VPC, KMS, and Lite Server modules.
Checks authentication through ModelArts notebook sessions or local credential inputs.
Creates, lists, starts, stops, updates, and deletes ModelArts notebooks and inference services.
Creates and manages training jobs using command mode or OBS script paths.
Queries VPC, subnet, security group, SWR image, KMS keypair, flavor, image, and resource pool data.
Manages Lite Server instances, hyper clusters, hyperinstances, volumes, EIPs, jobs, plugins, and software metadata.
limitations: Requires valid Huawei Cloud ModelArts credentials and account permissions.
Can change or delete cloud resources, so it is not suitable for untrusted prompts.
Some operations depend on Huawei Cloud SDK packages and regional service availability.
The skill does not provide a separate approval layer for destructive or costly actions.

use_cases.title: Operate ModelArts workspaces
use_cases.description: List notebooks, inspect services, manage resource pools, and coordinate routine workspace operations from an assistant session.

use_cases.title: Launch training workflows
use_cases.description: Create training jobs with selected images, resource pools, OBS paths, and job parameters after credentials are configured.

use_cases.title: Audit cloud dependencies
use_cases.description: Query VPC, subnet, security group, KMS keypair, SWR image, flavor, and Lite Server data before changing infrastructure.

prompt_templates.title: List my notebooks
prompt_templates.prompt: List my ModelArts notebooks in workspace 0 and summarize their names, status, owner, flavor, and creation time.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a training job
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a ModelArts training job. First check available images and resource pools, then ask before submitting the job.

prompt_templates.title: Review inference services
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my ModelArts inference services and identify stopped, failed, or unhealthy services. Do not modify resources.

prompt_templates.title: Plan pool maintenance
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a maintenance plan for ModelArts pools and Lite Server resources. Include dependencies, impact, and approval points before changes.

output_examples.output: A concise table of notebook names, IDs, statuses, flavors, owners, and next actions for stopped or failed instances.

output_examples.input: Prepare a training job for my custom image.
output_examples.output: A preflight summary showing selected image, resource pool, command or script mode, OBS paths, estimated impact, and required confirmation.

output_examples.input: Check VPC and security group readiness for a new pool.
output_examples.output: A network readiness summary with matching VPCs, subnets, security groups, open ports, and configuration risks to review.

best_practices: Use least-privilege Huawei Cloud credentials scoped to the workspace and services needed.
Ask for explicit confirmation before create, update, delete, start, stop, resize, or command-based training operations.
Run read-only inventory prompts first so the assistant can show IDs, regions, and expected impact before changes.
anti_patterns: Do not run this skill with administrator credentials for routine notebook or training tasks.
Do not paste long shell commands into training jobs without reviewing the exact command and image.
Do not allow untrusted user prompts to trigger destructive ModelArts or Lite Server actions.

faq.question: Does this skill require Huawei Cloud credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. It needs ModelArts notebook session credentials or local AK, SK, project, and region values.

faq.question: Can it create or delete cloud resources?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes lifecycle functions for notebooks, training jobs, inference services, pools, authorizations, and Lite Server resources.

faq.question: Is it read-only by default?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains both read-only query functions and write operations that can change resources.

faq.question: Can it run commands?
faq.answer: It can submit command strings to ModelArts training jobs. Review those commands before submission.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: The skill combines credentials, network API calls, destructive cloud operations, and remote training commands.
